title: mount: allow mount only when using vfs
body:
when using a driver different than vfs, the mount is probably in a
different mount namespace thus not accessible from the host.  Avoid
the confusion by not allowing mount when a different driver is used.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1225

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@giuseppe gofmt error, be sure to run gofmt 1.11 or higher.
```
# Run gofmt on version 1.11 and higher
Error: source files are not formatted according to recommendations. Run "gofmt -s -w" on:
./cmd/buildah/unshare_unsupported.go
```

Can I do a 
```
buildah unshare
mnt=$(buildah mount $ctr)
dnf -y install installroot=$mnt 
cp foo $mnt
buildah umount $mnt
exit
buildah commit $ctr imagename
buildah push imagename foobar
```

This is probably a reason we should allow mechanisms for buildah to specify whether or not it wants to use the OverlayFS when running as nonroot.

> Can I do a
> 
> ```
> buildah unshare
> mnt=$(buildah mount $ctr)
> dnf -y install installroot=$mnt 
> cp foo $mnt
> buildah umount $mnt
> exit
> buildah commit $ctr imagename
> buildah push imagename foobar
> ```
> 
> This is probably a reason we should allow mechanisms for buildah to specify whether or not it wants to use the OverlayFS when running as nonroot.

yes, you can do that.

Yes, in general VFS is safer, slower but safer.  Fortunately Buildah allows to use both storages so one can always switch between overlay and vfs.

Do you think it would make sense to add this fix also to ansible so that people are able to use the buildah connection plugin with rootless+ovl? I tried to do `buildah unshare` as root, and it seemed to work correctly -- so maybe the unshare could be the default for running commands in that ansible plugin both root and rootless.

> Do you think it would make sense to add this fix also to ansible so that people are able to use the buildah connection plugin with rootless+ovl? I tried to do `buildah unshare` as root, and it seemed to work correctly -- so maybe the unshare could be the default for running commands in that ansible plugin both root and rootless.

root won't need to do an unshare, as the root user is able to create mounts on the host namespace.

About rootless, it is only requires for `mount` and manually operating on the mounted directory.  It is not required for commands like `add` and `copy`.

Also it is not required when using `bud` as implicitly it does the unshare and operates all the commands in the new created namespaces.

@giuseppe Why did you have to add the fully qualified PRs?

Also we need to document this change in the man page, and tell people how they could do the buildah mount command while not root.
@rhatdan tests were failing as buildah couldn't find the image on registry.access.redhat.com
@giuseppe You want me to merge and then we can fix the man pages separately.
@rhatdan I've added another commit to update the man page

:pushpin: Commit a37389d has been approved by `rhatdan`
